Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 253. The Conquest of Marqasi (CT 53 878)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

[x x x x x x x]-u-ni

o 2'2'

[ki-i an-ni]-⸢i⸣ aq-ṭi-ba-šú

(2) I said to him [as foll]ows:

o 3'3'

[mu-ku x x x] ú-ma-a ina ŠÀ-bi-ka [o]

(3) "[......] Now consider within yourself

o 4'4'

[x x x x x]-tú ša KUR—aš-šur.KI

(4) [......] of Assyria

o 5'5'

[x x x x x] LÚv.NAM-MEŠ ša TAv ⸢LUGAL⸣

(5) [......] the governors who [went] with the king

o 6'6'

[x x x x] ⸢ša⸣ URU.mar-qa-si

(6) [......] of Marqasa

o 7'7'

[x x x x x]+⸢x⸣-ta-an-ni

(7) [......] ...

o 8'8'

[x x x x x x] KUR—aš-šur it-tab-lu

(8) [......] carried away [... of] Assyria

o 9'9'

[x x ina pa-ni-šú]-nu i-da-gal

(9) [......] is waiting [for th]em

o 10'10'

[x x x x x x]-áš-u-ni LUGAL É IGI-šú [o]

(10) [......] wherever the king [sets] his eye

o 11'11'

[x x x x x x x] ⸢x x x⸣ [x x x]

other side completely broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P314287/.
